title: Wide Dresser with Open Bottom Shelf
summary: A 57"-wide five-drawer dresser with an open display shelf across the bottom instead of a sixth drawer bay — three small drawers over two large ones, all on euro slides.
description:
The carcass is a plywood box on a 2x2 frame — two plywood sides, a plywood top, and an open bottom shelf that stays exposed rather than being boxed in behind a drawer. **That open shelf is the whole point of this piece**: it's built as an open-bottom dresser, not a six-drawer dresser with the last bay left empty, so the shelf front stays flush and unframed rather than trimmed like a drawer opening.

1x2 face-frame trim runs around the front and top of the carcass, with 2x2 rails front and back tying the sides together and giving the drawers something to run against. A 1/4" plywood back panel closes the whole thing in.

Three small drawers sit across the top, and two large drawers fill the row below, all riding on 16" euro-style bottom-corner-mount slides — the same slide type used elsewhere in this catalog's board-and-batten dresser, but this piece's open-shelf bottom and 57" width are what set it apart.

materials.name: Euro-style bottom corner mount drawer slides, 16 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 18-3/4", one top at 57" and one open bottom shelf at 55-1/2" from the 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet. Cut eight side trim at 15-3/4", two rail trim at 55-1/2", six bottom shelf trim at 7-1/2", two top trim, sides at 15-3/4" and two top trim, front at 60" from the 1x2. Cut four front/back legs frame at 31-3/4", two side frame members at 15-3/4" and six front/back rails at 55-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut one back panel at 58-1/2" from the 1/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet. Cut six small drawer sides at 16", six small drawer front/back at 15-1/2", four large drawer sides at 16" and four large drawer front/back at 24-7/8" from the 1x6. Cut three small drawer bottoms at 16" from the 1/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet. Cut three small drawer faces at 17-3/4" and two large drawer faces at 27-1/8" from the 1x8. Cut two large drawer bottoms at 26-3/8" from the 1/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to five 8 ft 1x2, six 8 ft 2x2, four 8 ft 1x6 and one 10 ft 1x8. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the carcass sides
steps.body: Attach the side trim to the two plywood sides.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Assemble the front/back leg frame, side frame members, and front/back rails, joining them to the two sides.

steps.title: Add the top and open shelf
steps.body: Attach the top with its trim, and the open bottom shelf with its trim across the front and sides — leave the shelf front flush and unframed.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Nail the plywood back panel across the full width and height of the carcass.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Mount the five euro-style drawer slides at their marked positions — three for the small drawers, two for the large drawers.
materials: Euro-style bottom corner mount drawer slides, 16 in

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Assemble the five drawer boxes from their sides, front/back, and plywood bottoms, then attach the drawer faces.

steps.title: Add hardware and finish
steps.body: Attach knobs or handles to each drawer, then sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Wide dresser with open bottom shelf and five drawers
